{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Zonal Coordinator",
  "description": "Vodstra Limited - Our client, a leading interstate passenger transport operator in Nigeria, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Zonal Coordinator

Job Locations: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking an experienced and results-driven Zonal Coordinator to oversee and drive operational excellence across assigned regions. We are specifically looking for candidates with strong experience managing large-scale transport operations within Nigeria&rsquo;s interstate passenger transport industry.


Job Responsibilities
This position is strictly for candidates who have:


	Held senior operational leadership roles within one of Nigeria&rsquo;s leading interstate passenger transport companies
	Managed multiple terminals, fleet operations, interstate routes, and high passenger volumes across assigned zones
	Successfully operated in fast-paced, highly regulated, and performance-driven environments
	Demonstrated strong field leadership, operational discipline, and team management capabilities
	Candidates without significant experience in large-scale interstate transport operations should not apply.


As Zonal Coordinator, you will be responsible for:


	Coordinating interstate transport operations across assigned zones and terminals
	Monitoring fleet utilization, route efficiency, passenger service delivery, and operational compliance
	Driving operational efficiency, revenue growth, and cost control initiatives within the zone
	Identifying and resolving operational bottlenecks, revenue leakages, and service gaps
	Supervising terminal managers, operations teams, and field personnel to ensure KPI achievement
	Managing relationships with regulators, unions, security agencies, and local stakeholders
	Ensuring adherence to company safety standards, operational policies, and service procedures
	Supporting business expansion and operational improvements within the assigned region


Candidate Profile


	Candidates should possess an HND, Bachelor&#39;s or Master&#39;s Degree
	8&ndash;12+ years of progressive experience in passenger transport operations
	Previous experience as a Zonal Manager, Regional Operations Manager, Operations Supervisor, or equivalent role within a reputable interstate passenger transport company
	Proven ability to manage multi-location transport operations effectively
	Strong understanding of Nigerian transport regulations and operational realities
	Excellent leadership, coordination, and problem-solving skills
	Commercially aware with strong operational reporting and analytical capabilities
	Results-oriented, disciplined, and execution-focused
	Experience managing large operational teams across different locations
	Willingness to travel frequently within assigned regions.


What We Offer


	Competitive compensation package tied to performance
	Opportunity to lead operations within a reputable national transport organization
	A platform to make measurable operational impact and grow within the business


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: info@vodstra.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-11",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }

Zonal Coordinator Job at Vodstra Limited




        
    

        
        Vodstra Limited - Our client, a leading interstate passenger transport operator in Nigeria, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Zonal Coordinator

Job Locations: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced and results-driven Zonal Coordinator to oversee and drive operational excellence across assigned regions. We are specifically looking for candidates with strong experience managing large-scale transport operations within Nigeria’s interstate passenger transport industry.


Job Responsibilities
This position is strictly for candidates who have:


	Held senior operational leadership roles within one of Nigeria’s leading interstate passenger transport companies
	Managed multiple terminals, fleet operations, interstate routes, and high passenger volumes across assigned zones
	Successfully operated in fast-paced, highly regulated, and performance-driven environments
	Demonstrated strong field leadership, operational discipline, and team management capabilities
	Candidates without significant experience in large-scale interstate transport operations should not apply.


As Zonal Coordinator, you will be responsible for:


	Coordinating interstate transport operations across assigned zones and terminals
	Monitoring fleet utilization, route efficiency, passenger service delivery, and operational compliance
	Driving operational efficiency, revenue growth, and cost control initiatives within the zone
	Identifying and resolving operational bottlenecks, revenue leakages, and service gaps
	Supervising terminal managers, operations teams, and field personnel to ensure KPI achievement
	Managing relationships with regulators, unions, security agencies, and local stakeholders
	Ensuring adherence to company safety standards, operational policies, and service procedures
	Supporting business expansion and operational improvements within the assigned region


Candidate Profile


	Candidates should possess an HND, Bachelor's or Master's Degree
	8–12+ years of progressive experience in passenger transport operations
	Previous experience as a Zonal Manager, Regional Operations Manager, Operations Supervisor, or equivalent role within a reputable interstate passenger transport company
	Proven ability to manage multi-location transport operations effectively
	Strong understanding of Nigerian transport regulations and operational realities
	Excellent leadership, coordination, and problem-solving skills
	Commercially aware with strong operational reporting and analytical capabilities
	Results-oriented, disciplined, and execution-focused
	Experience managing large operational teams across different locations
	Willingness to travel frequently within assigned regions.


What We Offer


	Competitive compensation package tied to performance
	Opportunity to lead operations within a reputable national transport organization
	A platform to make measurable operational impact and grow within the business


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Front Desk Officer",
  "description": "Stockgap Fuels Limited is a leading oil and gas company operating in Nigeria&rsquo;s downstream sector with head office in Port Harcourt, Rivers State. To meet the growing demand of Nigeria&rsquo;s Market, Stockgap Fuels Limited is expanding its LPG business by investing on additional storage and cylinder filling facility in 4 strategic locations spanning major regions of the country - Lagos/South-West, South-South/South-East, North-Central/North-West and North-East regions respectively.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	As the Front Desk Officer, you will be the first point of contact for visitors, clients, and stakeholders.
	You will manage reception operations, provide administrative support, and ensure smooth communication across departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Reception Management: Welcome and direct visitors with professionalism, manage the visitor waiting room.
	Communication Handling: Manage phone calls, mails, and correspondence.
	Visitor Coordination: Maintain visitor logs and enforce security protocols.
	Administrative Support: Assist with filing, scheduling, and document management.
	Meeting Assistance: Organize meeting rooms and logistics.
	Customer Service: Provide accurate information and direct inquiries appropriately.


Qualifications


	Education: OND/HND/Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Administration or related field.
	Experience: 2&ndash;3 years in front desk or administrative roles, preferably in oil and gas.
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in MS Office Suite and office equipment.
	Soft Skills: Strong interpersonal, organizational, and multitasking abilities.
	Industry Knowledge: Familiarity with downstream oil and gas operations and compliance standards is an advantage.


What we offer


	Competitive Salary: N200,000 - N350,000 per month and benefits package
	Professional growth opportunities
	A dynamic and supportive work environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified applicants should send their CV to: careers@stockgapfuels.com using &quot;Application &ndash; Front Desk Officer&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Front Desk Officer Job at Stockgap Fuels Limited




        
    

        
        Stockgap Fuels Limited is a leading oil and gas company operating in Nigeria’s downstream sector with head office in Port Harcourt, Rivers State. To meet the growing demand of Nigeria’s Market, Stockgap Fuels Limited is expanding its LPG business by investing on additional storage and cylinder filling facility in 4 strategic locations spanning major regions of the country - Lagos/South-West, South-South/South-East, North-Central/North-West and North-East regions respectively.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	As the Front Desk Officer, you will be the first point of contact for visitors, clients, and stakeholders.
	You will manage reception operations, provide administrative support, and ensure smooth communication across departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Reception Management: Welcome and direct visitors with professionalism, manage the visitor waiting room.
	Communication Handling: Manage phone calls, mails, and correspondence.
	Visitor Coordination: Maintain visitor logs and enforce security protocols.
	Administrative Support: Assist with filing, scheduling, and document management.
	Meeting Assistance: Organize meeting rooms and logistics.
	Customer Service: Provide accurate information and direct inquiries appropriately.


Qualifications


	Education: OND/HND/Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration or related field.
	Experience: 2–3 years in front desk or administrative roles, preferably in oil and gas.
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in MS Office Suite and office equipment.
	Soft Skills: Strong interpersonal, organizational, and multitasking abilities.
	Industry Knowledge: Familiarity with downstream oil and gas operations and compliance standards is an advantage.


What we offer


	Competitive Salary: N200,000 - N350,000 per month and benefits package
	Professional growth opportunities
	A dynamic and supportive work environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified applicants should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Application – Front Desk Officer" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Chief Security Officer",
  "description": "Stockgap Fuels Limited - We are a leading oil and gas company operating in Nigeria&rsquo;s downstream sector with head office in Port Harcourt, Rivers State. To meet the growing demand of Nigeria&rsquo;s Market, Stockgap Fuels Limited is expanding its LPG business by investing on additional storage and cylinder filling facility in 4 strategic locations spanning major regions of the country - Lagos/South-West, South-South/South-East, North-Central/North-West and North-East regions respectively.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Security Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Purpose of the Role


	The Chief Security Officer [CSO] is responsible for developing and executing the company&rsquo;s security strategy. This includes physical security of plants and depots, logistics and fleet security, executive protection, investigations, intelligence, and cyber/IT security governance.
	The Chief Security Officer partners with Operations, CISCO, and HR to minimize risk and enable safe, uninterrupted business operations.


Main Duties and Responsibilities
Security Strategy &amp; Governance:


	Develop and implement an enterprise-wide security policy and framework aligned to ISO 27001, ISO 22301, and industry best practices.
	Define security KPIs, risk appetite, and reporting cadence for Exco and the Board.
	Manage the annual security budget and resource allocation.


Physical &amp; Perimeter Security:


	Oversee security operations at all plants, depots, micro-hubs, and offices.
	Ensure effective access control, CCTV, alarm systems, and guarding services.
	Conduct regular security audits, risk assessments, and vulnerability tests.


Intelligence &amp; Incident Management:


	Establish threat intelligence gathering on host communities, political, and criminal risks.
	Lead incident response, crisis management, and business continuity protocols.
	Conduct investigations into theft, fraud, sabotage, and workplace violence.


Cybersecurity Oversight:


	Partner with CTO to define cybersecurity policies, data protection, and IT risk controls.
	Oversee penetration testing, employee awareness training, and incident response for cyber events.
	Ensure compliance with NDPR and other data protection regulations.


Regulatory &amp; Community Compliance:


	Liaise with Police, DSS, NSCDC, military units, and local security networks.
	Ensure compliance with Nigeria&rsquo;s security regulations and host community agreements.
	Advise Corporate Affairs on security aspects of community engagement.


People &amp; Culture:


	Build and lead a high-performing security team across regions.
	Deliver security awareness and safety training for staff and contractors.
	Foster a culture of vigilance, reporting, and accountability.


Logistics &amp; Fleet Security:


	Design and monitor security protocols for product transportation, including convoy management, route risk analysis, and tracking systems.
	Coordinate with Operations and 3PLs to prevent hijacking, pilferage, and route disruptions.


Qualifications, Experience &amp; Competencies


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Security Management, Criminology, Law Enforcement, Engineering, or related field.
	12-15 years of experience in corporate security, military, police, or intelligence, with 5+ years in a national leadership role.
	Master&rsquo;s/MBA is an advantage.
	Experience in oil &amp; gas, logistics, or critical infrastructure security in Nigeria.
	Strong knowledge of physical security systems, investigations, and risk management.


Key Skills &amp; Abilities:


	Strategic thinking and crisis management under pressure.
	Strong leadership, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Analytical skills for threat assessment and incident investigation.
	Familiarity with security technologies: CCTV, access control, GPS tracking, SIEM.
	Knowledge of Nigerian security landscape, laws, and regulatory bodies.
	High integrity, discretion, and ability to handle sensitive information.


Salary
N1,000,000 - N2,500,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified applicants should send their CV to: careers@stockgapfuels.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Chief Security Officer Job at Stockgap Fuels Limited




        
    

        
        Stockgap Fuels Limited - We are a leading oil and gas company operating in Nigeria’s downstream sector with head office in Port Harcourt, Rivers State. To meet the growing demand of Nigeria’s Market, Stockgap Fuels Limited is expanding its LPG business by investing on additional storage and cylinder filling facility in 4 strategic locations spanning major regions of the country - Lagos/South-West, South-South/South-East, North-Central/North-West and North-East regions respectively.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Security Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Purpose of the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Chief Security Officer [CSO] is responsible for developing and executing the company’s security strategy. This includes physical security of plants and depots, logistics and fleet security, executive protection, investigations, intelligence, and cyber/IT security governance.
	The Chief Security Officer partners with Operations, CISCO, and HR to minimize risk and enable safe, uninterrupted business operations.


Main Duties and Responsibilities
Security Strategy & Governance:


	Develop and implement an enterprise-wide security policy and framework aligned to ISO 27001, ISO 22301, and industry best practices.
	Define security KPIs, risk appetite, and reporting cadence for Exco and the Board.
	Manage the annual security budget and resource allocation.


Physical & Perimeter Security:


	Oversee security operations at all plants, depots, micro-hubs, and offices.
	Ensure effective access control, CCTV, alarm systems, and guarding services.
	Conduct regular security audits, risk assessments, and vulnerability tests.


Intelligence & Incident Management:


	Establish threat intelligence gathering on host communities, political, and criminal risks.
	Lead incident response, crisis management, and business continuity protocols.
	Conduct investigations into theft, fraud, sabotage, and workplace violence.


Cybersecurity Oversight:


	Partner with CTO to define cybersecurity policies, data protection, and IT risk controls.
	Oversee penetration testing, employee awareness training, and incident response for cyber events.
	Ensure compliance with NDPR and other data protection regulations.


Regulatory & Community Compliance:


	Liaise with Police, DSS, NSCDC, military units, and local security networks.
	Ensure compliance with Nigeria’s security regulations and host community agreements.
	Advise Corporate Affairs on security aspects of community engagement.


People & Culture:


	Build and lead a high-performing security team across regions.
	Deliver security awareness and safety training for staff and contractors.
	Foster a culture of vigilance, reporting, and accountability.


Logistics & Fleet Security:


	Design and monitor security protocols for product transportation, including convoy management, route risk analysis, and tracking systems.
	Coordinate with Operations and 3PLs to prevent hijacking, pilferage, and route disruptions.


Qualifications, Experience & Competencies


	Bachelor’s Degree in Security Management, Criminology, Law Enforcement, Engineering, or related field.
	12-15 years of experience in corporate security, military, police, or intelligence, with 5+ years in a national leadership role.
	Master’s/MBA is an advantage.
	Experience in oil & gas, logistics, or critical infrastructure security in Nigeria.
	Strong knowledge of physical security systems, investigations, and risk management.


Key Skills & Abilities:


	Strategic thinking and crisis management under pressure.
	Strong leadership, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Analytical skills for threat assessment and incident investigation.
	Familiarity with security technologies: CCTV, access control, GPS tracking, SIEM.
	Knowledge of Nigerian security landscape, laws, and regulatory bodies.
	High integrity, discretion, and ability to handle sensitive information.


Salary
N1,000,000 - N2,500,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified applicants should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Executive",
  "description": "At Soft Intelligent Machines Limited, we are a team of professionals and technical specialists from wide-ranging backgrounds. We combine the capacity to promote businesses, deliver services, and build organizations, as well as strengthen their systems, and have sustained our reputation for improving organizational cultures and business outcomes. Our charter is to focus on evolving innovative solutions for change at affordable costs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Soft Intelligent Machines is seeking an experienced Sales Executive to join our innovative team.
	This role is ideal for professionals with a passion for technology, sales, and problem-solving.
	The successful candidate will support the sales process by understanding client needs, providing product insights, and helping deliver solutions that drive business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Identifying and developing new business opportunities within the IT space.
	Work closely with the technical and support teams in preparing proposals, quotations, and client presentations.
	Participate in product demonstrations, client meetings, and technical discussions.
	Learn and communicate the value of our technology solutions to clients in clear, business-focused terms.
	Maintain records of client interactions and follow up to ensure satisfaction and relationship growth.
	Stay up to date on company products, industry trends, and competitor offerings.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Computer Science, Marketing, or any related field.
	2 - 4 years of experience in IT, sales, marketing, or technical support.
	Previous experience in IT sales is preferred
	Demonstrable track record of over-achieving quota
	Ability to build and maintain client relationships.
	Target-driven with a strong passion for sales.
	IT knowledge.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: christianah@softintelligentmachines.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Sales Executive Job at Soft Intelligent Machines Limited




        
    

        
        At Soft Intelligent Machines Limited, we are a team of professionals and technical specialists from wide-ranging backgrounds. We combine the capacity to promote businesses, deliver services, and build organizations, as well as strengthen their systems, and have sustained our reputation for improving organizational cultures and business outcomes. Our charter is to focus on evolving innovative solutions for change at affordable costs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Soft Intelligent Machines is seeking an experienced Sales Executive to join our innovative team.
	This role is ideal for professionals with a passion for technology, sales, and problem-solving.
	The successful candidate will support the sales process by understanding client needs, providing product insights, and helping deliver solutions that drive business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Identifying and developing new business opportunities within the IT space.
	Work closely with the technical and support teams in preparing proposals, quotations, and client presentations.
	Participate in product demonstrations, client meetings, and technical discussions.
	Learn and communicate the value of our technology solutions to clients in clear, business-focused terms.
	Maintain records of client interactions and follow up to ensure satisfaction and relationship growth.
	Stay up to date on company products, industry trends, and competitor offerings.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Marketing, or any related field.
	2 - 4 years of experience in IT, sales, marketing, or technical support.
	Previous experience in IT sales is preferred
	Demonstrable track record of over-achieving quota
	Ability to build and maintain client relationships.
	Target-driven with a strong passion for sales.
	IT knowledge.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

        Macden Communications Limited is into the sales, supply and distribution of beverages and is a major distributor for International Breweries PLC, Nigerian Breweries Limited and Guiness Nigerian Limited.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chartered Accountant

Job Location: Iyana Ipaja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Working Days and Time: 8am to 6pm and from Mondays to Saturdays and Public Holidays

Responsibilities


	Financial Reporting.
	Auditing.
	Budgeting Analysis.
	Internal Control.
	Others.


Requirements

Salary
N350,000 - N400,000 per month.
